title: Exam-slot capacity gate on booking (computed seats/schools vs a manual flag)
business_reason: SPEC_VS_BUILT_GAP exam_slots: 'capacity rules absent'. A school could book an exam slot beyond its seat capacity — the existing capacity_gate_status was a manual flag, never computed. Gate the booking create on the slot's REAL capacity (sum of active bookings' confirmed_student_count vs capacity_students; distinct schools vs capacity_schools).

design.pure_helper: server/lib/slotCapacity.ts checkSlotCapacity({capacityStudents, capacitySchools, bookedStudents, bookedSchoolIds, newSchoolId, newCount}) -> {ok, reason}. Seat + school limits; a re-booking school doesn't re-count; rejects non-positive counts. Unit-tested.
design.create_guard: server/lib/createGuards.ts assertCreateAllowed(table, payload, actor) — table-keyed create precondition (mirrors computeOnCreate). exam_slot_bookings: load the slot + its active bookings, run checkSlotCapacity, throw ValidationError (-> 422) if over.
design.kernel: createModuleRecord calls assertCreateAllowed before any persistence (dynamic import to avoid a circular dep). Generalizable — other create-time gates add a table branch.

param_14.question: How is exam-slot overbooking prevented?
param_14.answer: When a school books a slot, the server computes the slot's current load (sum of active bookings' confirmed students + distinct schools) and rejects the booking if it would exceed capacity_students or capacity_schools. The check is real (from bookings), not a manual flag; counts are server-side.
param_14.security_implications: fail-closed capacity gate at create time (before persistence); counts only ACTIVE bookings (reserved/confirmed/locked, not cancelled); the gate is server-computed, not a client-trusted flag; generalizable create-guard hook keeps the kernel the single enforcement point.
param_14.approval_implications: none new; over-capacity bookings are blocked, not queued.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: complements the slot-assignment capacity_gate (chain5); a waitlist/over-allocation override is a follow-up. School-cap path is unit-tested (one dev school in e2e).
param_14.assumptions: one booking row per (school, slot) booking; capacity_students/schools are set on the slot.
